title: Remove redundant inline comments that restate code
description: client_task.rs contains inline comments that describe what the next line does rather than why. Specifically: line 592 '// Decompose Unix timestamp into date/time components' precedes straightforward modular arithmetic that is already self-evident from variable names (s, m, h, days). This pattern adds noise without adding understanding. Comments should explain intent, invariants, or non-obvious choices — not restate the operation. Audit and remove comments that a reader could derive by reading the line itself. Keep comments that explain *why* (e.g. the leap-year epoch shift note, the Hinnant reference link).
close_reason: Stale: the now_rfc3339 function that contained hand-rolled timestamp decomposition was replaced with chrono::Utc::now().format(); the offending comments no longer exist
